per_question.user_input:
How to resolve the StaleConnectionException in WebSphere Application Server? I see this exception in WebSphere Application Server J2c and RRA traces:

WSRdbManagedC > preTestConnection Entry WSRdbManagedConnectionImpl@51a507f2 query: SELECT 1 FROM DUAL WSRdbManagedC 3 Exception java.sql.SQLException: ORA-02396: exceeded maximum idle time, please connect again DSRA0010E: SQL State = 61000, Error Code = 2,396 at oracle.jdbc.driver.T4CTTIoer.processError(T4CTTIoer.java:440) at 
...
Entry java.sql.SQLException: ORA-01012: not logged on ..... OracleDataSto < findMappingClass return Exit class com.ibm.websphere.ce.cm.StaleConnectionException
retrieved_contexts:
RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Steps to resolve the problem: 

1. Stopped WAS hosting the WorkplaceXT
2. Removed any additional jar files from the <install_path>/IBM/WebShpere/AppServer/lib/ext directory so that the directory contained only two jar files: 

- authentication-websphere.jar
- log4j_1.2.13.jar

3. Deleted WorkplaceXT folder from the WAS wstemp folder, and clear the browser cache
4. Started WebSphere Application Server

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Configure the WebSphere Application Server data source connection pool [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/wasinfo/v7r0/index.jsp?topic=/com.ibm.websphere.nd.multiplatform.doc/info/ae/ae/udat_conpoolset.html] so as to eliminate occurrences of StaleConnectionException :

CAUSE
Oracle requires that you have special permissions to attempt to perform the recovery.
If there is a system crash during a distributed transaction, the WebSphere Application Server transaction manager will attempt to clean up any failed transactions which it finds in the transaction logs. 
When the recovery involves an Oracle XAResource, the Oracle JDBC driver 
oracle.jdbc.xa.OracleXAResource.recover function will make a call to DIST_TXN_SYNC.

ENVIRONMENT
The exception com.ibm.websphere.ce.cm.StaleConnectionException is specific to WebSphere Application Server environments. But a similar issue can be experienced with Weblogic, see: Rule Execution Server on WebLogic unable to load ruleset after period of inactivity [http://www-01.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21615801]

If the J2CA0036E and DSRA0023E are logged in the application server's SystemOut.log, then check the JDBC Provider configuration to see if it uses WebSphere variables for its classpath setting. From the Administrative Console select Resources > JDBC Provider > DB_Provider_Used_By_DataSource, and inspect the classpath. For this example, one would check to see if the JDBC Provider configuration uses the Application Server's DB2_INSTALL_ROOT and DB2_JDBC_DRIVER_PATH variable.
per_question.response: To resolve the StaleConnectionException in WebSphere Application Server, configure the data source connection pool to eliminate occurrences of this exception. Additionally, ensure that the JDBC Provider configuration uses WebSphere variables for its classpath setting, as indicated by checking the classpath in the Administrative Console under Resources > JDBC Provider.
per_question.reference:
To resolve the StaleConnectionException in WebSphere Application Server, you need to set the "Validate on Reserve" property to true for the connection pool associated with the data source in WebSphere Application Server. This setting will ensure that the connections are validated before being returned to the application to prevent stale connections. 

Additionally, you may need to adjust the maximum idle time setting in the Oracle database to prevent the ORA-02396 error. This can be done by modifying the SQLNET.ORA file on the Oracle server to increase the value of the SQLNET.EXPIRE_TIME parameter. 

By setting "Validate on Reserve" to true in the connection pool and adjusting the maximum idle time in the Oracle database, you should be able to resolve the StaleConnectionException in WebSphere Application Server.

retrieved_contexts:
CAUSE
WebSphere MQ (WMQ) Resource Adapter, shipped with WebSphere Application Server, had a defect which blocked the finalizer thread while closing a ServerSession object. This would then block the garbage collection of all objects with a finalize method, leading to OutOfMemory events. The largest heap user might be com.ibm.jsse2.SSLSocketImpl objects. This problem was fixed under WMQ APAR IV14093 which shipped with certain levels of WebSphere Application Server Fix Packs.

   hangs.
   
   The problem can be detected by observing the WSVR0605W hung
   thread notifications in the SystemOut.log.
   
   One thread will be running the FFDCFilter.processException()
   which can be invoked from any WebSphere Application Server
   component.  The thread will get stuck doing its work.
   In the following example, the thread which invoked

12C
   Java Stack
   at
   com/ibm/ejs/j2c/RALifeCycleManagerImpl.shutdown(RALifeCycleManag
   erImpl.java:1483)
   at
   com/ibm/ejs/j2c/RALifeCycleManagerImpl.stop(RALifeCycleManagerIm
   pl.java:782)
   at
   com/ibm/ws/runtime/component/ApplicationMgrImpl.stop(Application
   MgrImpl.java:1151)
   ...
   
   This is a timing/synchronization issue that can occur when
   running WebSphere Application Server with a fix pack level

WSDL or parsing the SOAP response message.
   
      This can lead to the following hung threads
   [4/14/16 8:01:33:763 CEST] 000000a5 ThreadMonitor W   WSVR0605W:
    Thread
   "WebContainer : 0" (000001b2) has been active for 726141
   milliseconds and may be hung.  There is/are 1 thread(s) in
   total in the server that may be hung.
           at
   java.util.HashMap.findNonNullKeyEntry(HashMap.java:605)
   
   
   "WebContainer : 37" J9VMThread:0x0000000049652700,

long as the thread is detected as hung, using the
   com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java.track property.
   If this property is unset, the default value is set to be
   identical to the value of
   com.ibm.websphere.threadmonitor.dump.java.  If the property is

per_question.response: To resolve a WSVR0605W hung thread issue in the WebSphere MQ Resource Adapter, you should first ensure that you are using a version of WebSphere Application Server that includes the fix for the defect identified in WMQ APAR IV14093, which addresses the blocking of the finalizer thread. Additionally, monitor the thread activity and consider adjusting the thread monitor properties to detect and manage hung threads effectively.
per_question.reference:
To resolve a WSVR0605W hung thread issue in the WebSphere MQ Resource Adapter, you need to tune the thread pool size for the WebSphere MQ Resource Adapter. By default, the WebSphere MQ Resource Adapter thread pool is created with a minimum of 2 threads and a maximum of 20 threads. The Growable option is set to true, allowing the number of threads to go beyond the maximum if needed. To prevent blocked threads, you may need to limit and tune the maximum thread pool size. Individual performance tuning in a pre-production test environment is recommended to avoid performance problems on a production server.

You can access the configuration for work managers in the administrative console under Resources > Asynchronous beans > Work managers > WMQJCAResourceAdapter. It is important to configure work managers according to your specific requirements and workload to prevent hung thread issues.

How can I export a private key from DataPower Gateway Appliance? 

How can I export a private key from DataPower Gateway appliance?
retrieved_contexts:
TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 How do I export and import private keys between the same or different Hardware Security Module (HSM) enabled IBM WebSphere DataPower SOA Appliance? 

ANSWER
HSM-enabled DataPower appliances support the export of private keys using the crypto-export command. For key export to work, various conditions must be met:

Second, the key to be exported must be exportable. The exportability of keys is immutable. It is determined at keygen time, and it is controlled by that command's exportable parameter. If a key was created outside of the appliance (not using keygen), then it is always considered exportable.

As for the certificate configuration on DataPower, the appliance will reference the actual key and certificate files stored in the cert: directory by file name. Navigation in the WebGUI to the SSL Proxy Profile -> Crypto Profile -> Crypto Key and Certificate objects will show the current files referenced by the configuration.

[/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21500046&aid=3] [/support/docview.wss?uid=swg21500046&aid=3]

Once all of this has been done, then private keys may move from system to system with crypto-export and crypto-import. 

Note that the non-HSM appliance can export keys immediately at keygen time, but never at a later time. 

For additional information refer to the online Info Center [http://publib.boulder.ibm.com/infocenter/wsdatap/v3r8m2/index.jsp?topic=%2Fxi50%2Fwelcome.htm] and search for "PIN entry device".
. If the goal is to move exported keys from one appliance to another one, then all of the steps in this section must be followed :-
per_question.response: To export a private key from a DataPower Gateway appliance, you must use the crypto-export command, ensuring that the key is exportable, which is determined at key generation time. Additionally, you need to follow all specified steps for key export, including verifying the key's exportability and navigating to the appropriate configuration settings in the WebGUI.
per_question.reference:
To export a private key from DataPower Gateway Appliance, you can use the `crypto-export` command. However, to successfully export a private key, the following conditions must be met:

1. Ensure that the HSMs on both the exporting and importing machines are initialized and in the same key sharing domain.
2. The private key you want to export must be marked as exportable at the time of key generation.
3. The HSMs on both machines must share internal key-wrapping keys. These keys encrypt the exported private keys and decrypt imported private keys.
4. If you are moving keys from one appliance to another, synchronize the key-wrapping keys using the `hsm-clone-kwk` command on both appliances.

Follow these steps to successfully export a private key from a DataPower Gateway Appliance using the `crypto-export` command.

per_question.user_input: When should I use conversation sharing in DataPower MQ queue managers? MQ version 7.0 introduced a new feature, conversation sharing. Are there any special values on DataPower when configuring conversation sharing?
retrieved_contexts:
The default setting of sharing conversations (SHARECNV) value is zero in DataPower mq-qm object. The same default value for MQ SVRCONN channel is 10. The sharing conversations value is 
negotiated between MQ server and DataPower and the lower value takes effect. However, in current versions of the DataPower firmware, the sharing conversations setting of 1 is treated as 0 when negotiating with MQ server.

For DataPower firmware releases 7.0.0.x, 7.1.0.x, 7.2.0.x and earlier, one should follow case #1 and use a negotiated sharing conversations value of 0. If you configure these firmware versions with case #2 or case #3, DataPower device may reload or hang due to connection instability and generate many MQ errors with Reason Code - 2009 and 2059.

Set a value of 1 on the Sharing Conversations attribute and select "on" in Share Single Conversation attribute of the mq-qm object in DataPower as shown in the following picture and a value of 1 on IBM WebSphere MQ SVRCONN setting.

RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
IBM WebSphere MQ server version 7.0 and later releases provide sharing conversations (SHARECNV) attribute on SVRCONN channel that specifies the maximum number of conversations that can share each TCP/IP channel instance. This feature can be configured in DataPower as it uses client connection with queue manager SVRCONN channel.

There are three use cases to configure Sharing Conversations in DataPower mq-qm object: 

Case #1 : The negotiated sharing conversations value is 0 - The channel runs in a mode similar to WebSphere MQ V6 and does not uses features such as

· Administrator stop-quiesce
· Heartbeating 
· Read ahead 
· Client asynchronous consume
per_question.response: You should use conversation sharing in DataPower MQ queue managers when configuring with IBM WebSphere MQ server version 7.0 and later, as it allows for the maximum number of conversations to share each TCP/IP channel instance. However, for DataPower firmware releases 7.0.0.x, 7.1.0.x, 7.2.0.x and earlier, it is recommended to use a negotiated sharing conversations value of 0 to avoid connection instability and errors.
per_question.reference:
Conversation sharing in DataPower MQ queue managers should be used when negotiating the sharing conversations value. It is beneficial to configure the Sharing Conversations attribute with a specific value based on the negotiated value with the MQ server. 

Three different use cases for configuring sharing conversations in DataPower MQ queue managers are outlined:
1. When the negotiated sharing conversations value is 0, it is recommended to set a value of 0 or 1 on the Sharing Conversations attribute in DataPower to disable sharing conversations.
2. If the negotiated sharing conversations value is 1, it is advised to set a value of 1 on the Sharing Conversations attribute and select "on" in Share Single Conversation attribute in DataPower. 
3. When the negotiated sharing conversations value is 2 or more, it is recommended to set a value of 2 or more on the Sharing Conversations attribute.

It is important to configure the Sharing Conversations attribute appropriately to ensure optimal performance and avoid potential errors or instability in the MQ environment.

The cause of the problem is that the WebSphere Application Server Java™ virtual machine (JVM) could not find the JDBC JAR file, in this example the DB2 JAR, in its classpath that contains the implementation class. The most likely cause is that the classpath of the JDBC Provider configuration has used a WebSphere Application Server variable, for example, DB2_JDBC_DRIVER_PATH, which has not been set for the corresponding WebSphere Application Server jvm process
in IBM Business Process Manager (BPM) V8.5.7 cumulative fix 2016.06
per_question.reference: The file db2jcc.jar is missing after an upgrade to BPM V8.5.7 CF2016.12 because IBM BPM does not transform complex IBM BPM types (such as TWObjects) to standard JDBC types that the database can process. Therefore, the SQL Connector code can only handle simple types. This limitation was not documented previously, but it will be addressed in an upcoming IBM BPM V8.5.7 cumulative fix by adding information about this limitation to the SQLParameter variable types description. In the meantime, a temporary fix is to use the Java integration feature to invoke custom Java code using JDBC java interfaces to call stored procedures.

If you want your network traffic to follow a specific route, set a static route and NOT rely on the default gateway. As a general rule, the management interface should not have the default gateway set, as you generally want to restrict the connections that can be made through that interface. 

A general recommendation is to set specific static routes and only ONE default gateway that will catch all traffic not being directed by the static routes.

A system dump shows an abend 0C1 occurring in the Debug Tool for z/OS module EQA10OSM. 
I receive the following messages:
DFHAP1203I Language Environment is being initialized. 
DFHSR0601 Program interrupt occurred with system task III in control.
retrieved_contexts:
PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 You upgrade to a new release of z/OS and CICS Transaction Server fails to initialize. You see the following messages:

DFHAP1203I Language Environment is being initialized.
DFHSR0601 Program interrupt occurred with system task III in control. 

CAUSE
There is a mismatch in z/OS levels between Language Environment modules, CEECCICS and CEEPLPKA.

0C4 ABENDS0C4 BPXP018I DFHKE1800 MSGDFHSR0601 MSGDFHSR0622 5655M1500 R640 640 R400 400 R650 650 R500 500 HCI6400 HCI6500 5655-M15 5655M15 kixinfo retention32 TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 Your CICS Transaction Server for z/OS (CICS TS) region receives messages:
DFHSR0622 An attempt to overwrite the ERDSA has caused the abend which follows
DFHSR0601 Program interrupt occurred with system task TCP in control
DFHKE1800 ABNORMAL TERMINATION OF CICS IS COMPLETE

Now that you know the module name and the offset, search for a known problem on the CICS Support page [http://www.ibm.com/support/entry/portal/Overview/Software/Other_Software/CICS_Transaction_Server]. Use keywords such as the following for a search argument: 'DFHAP0001 DFHAPDM 0C4'. 

RELATED INFORMATION
 Finding where a program check occurred [http://www.ibm.com/support/knowledgecenter/SSGMCP_4.1.0/com.ibm.cics.ts.doc/dfhs1/topics/dfhs1l8.html]

CECI ABEND0C4 ABEND 0C4 ABENDS0C4 ABEND S0C4 DFHSR0001 MSGDFHSR0001 EXECKEY 5655M1500 R640 640 R400 400 R650 650 R500 500 HCI6400 HCI6500 5655-M15 5655M15 kixinfo retention32 TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING)

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 You receive the following message in your CICS Transaction Server region: 

DFHSR0001 An abend (code 0C4/AKEA) has occurred at offset X'B0E' in program DFHECID. 

CAUSE
The startup GRPLIST contains a duplicate definition for program DFHECID with specification EXECKEY(USER).

PROBLEM(ABSTRACT)
 You receive a program check that has an offset X'FFFFFFFF' in a CICS Transaction Server for z/OS (CICS TS) V4.1 region. For example, DFHAP0001 An abend (code 0C4/AKEA) has occurred at offset X'FFFFFFFF' in module DFHAMP. The X'FFFFFFFF' indicates that CICS was unable establish the location of the program check. So, you would like to locate the PSW and registers in your CICS system dump.
per_question.response: The CICS region is failing during startup with message DFHAP1203I followed by DFHSR0601 due to a mismatch in z/OS levels between Language Environment modules, CEECCICS and CEEPLPKA. This mismatch can lead to program interrupts, as indicated by the DFHSR0601 message, which suggests that the initialization process is encountering issues related to the environment setup.
per_question.reference: The issue is occurring due to a mismatch in z/OS levels between Language Environment modules, specifically CEECCICS and CEEPLPKA. To resolve this problem, ensure that there is only one instance of CEEPLPKA and CEECCICS in your library concatenations, and that they are at the correct z/OS level.

per_question.reference:
Yes, the BPM internal Document Store can work with IBM DB2 pureScale. If you are experiencing hanging threads during startup of the server and initialization of the internal document store, it may be due to issues with the pureScale setup and configuration. 

One potential solution is to ensure that the DB2 JCC properties for DB2 pureScale affinity are correctly configured on Tomcat. This involves setting pureScale member host names in the operating system's hosts file, adding JNDI resource information to the web application environment file, adding JNDI data source information to the Tomcat configuration file, and implementing custom code to retrieve supported user attributes from the underlying security repository.

By correctly configuring the DB2 JCC properties for DB2 pureScale affinity on Tomcat, you may be able to resolve the hanging threads issue during BPM server startup and internal document store initialization.

per_question.response: The Java.io.NotSerializableException you are encountering, indicated by the error message WTRN0047E, typically occurs when an object that does not implement the java.io.Serializable or java.io.Externalizable interface is placed in a session. This often involves attributes within the session object or objects referenced by those attributes, leading to repeated NotSerializableException entries in the server log.
per_question.reference:
Based on the context provided, the Java.io.NotSerializableException typically occurs when an object that does not implement the `java.io.Serializable` interface is attempted to be serialized or deserialized. This exception can be encountered in scenarios involving Persistent sessions, PMI, and HTTPSession.

To resolve the `java.io.NotSerializableException`, ensure that all objects placed in the HTTPSession implement the `java.io.Serializable` interface. Additionally, you can use the `SessionInspectServlet` provided by WebSphere Application Server to determine which attribute of the session object references a non-serializable object.

If you are experiencing this exception in WAS, it is recommended to check the objects being stored in the session for serialization compatibility and use the `SessionInspectServlet` to identify the issue further and ensure proper implementation of the `java.io.Serializable` interface for all relevant objects.

per_question.response: Your JVMSERVER remains in a "BEING DISABLED" state because the Language Environment Enclave was not created, which leads to the server being disabled as indicated by the DFHSJ0914 message. Additionally, if zFS files are not propagated to the target environment, it can cause the JVM Server to fail to initialize the OSGi framework, resulting in a hung state.
per_question.reference:
Based on the provided context, the issue with the JVMSERVER remaining in a "BEING DISABLED" state could be due to the zFS files not being properly propagated to the target environments after applying preventative maintenance to CICS Transaction Server for z/OS. When the updated zFS files are not rolled out to the target environments, it can impact the functioning of the JVM Servers.

To resolve this issue, ensure that the updated zFS files are properly propagated to all target environments when maintenance is applied to CICS. This should allow the JVMSERVER to be disabled successfully without getting stuck in a "BEING DISABLED" state.

per_question.user_input: Does IBM DataPower Gateway Appliance support the HTTP CONNECT method? Does IBM DataPower Gateway Appliance support the HTTP CONNECT method for SSL proxy requests?
retrieved_contexts:
ERROR DESCRIPTION
 *  Datapower service (i.e MPGW, WSP, etc) is configured to connect
   to backend server through an HTTP Proxy server (as configured
   in the Proxy Policy of the user agent) using the HTTP CONNECT
   method.
   However, if the HTTP CONNECT request to the proxy server,
   receives a 5xx response, Datapower might issue the following SSL
   error:
   20160711T163325.760Z [0x8120002f][ssl][error]
   ssl-client(PMYClientProfile): tid(11573565)[10.x.x.x]

Users configuring DataPower may need a way to implement this CONNECT method for SSL tunneling to a remote Proxy Server.




RESOLVING THE PROBLEM
Non-SSL Proxy requests will work using either:

a) an XML Firewall when configuring in the HTTP Options tab, where the user can configure the Proxy Host and Port

b) a Multi-Protocol Gateway when configuring an HTTP Proxy Policy in the User Agent

PROBLEM SUMMARY
 *  Affected are all customers using the Multi-Protocol Gateway,
   Web Service Proxy or Web Application Firewall with persistent
   connection to the backend side.
   
   In case the backend server stops responding to HTTP GET
   requests while there are already some established TCP
   connections from the time the server worked OK, DataPower will
   automatically retry those GET requests after a timeout period.

b) a Multi-Protocol Gateway when configuring an HTTP Proxy Policy in the User Agent

SSL Proxy requests will work using the CONNECT method for SSL Tunneling when using a Multi-Protocol Gateway when configuring in a User Agent's HTTP Proxy Policy.

The XML Firewall service is not designed to use the CONNECT method for SSL Tunneling.

For DataPower versions 6.0.0 and later:

TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA
TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA
TLS_RSA_WITH_NULL_SHA256
TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA256
TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA256

For DataPower versions prior to 6.0.0:

TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_128_CBC_SHA
TLS_RSA_WITH_AES_256_CBC_SHA
AES_SHA_US

(b). In WebSphere MQ channels select the same TLS protocol as DataPower uses from the channel CipherSpec.

When DataPower mq-qm object is using an SSL Proxy Profile:
per_question.response: Yes, IBM DataPower Gateway Appliance supports the HTTP CONNECT method for SSL proxy requests when using a Multi-Protocol Gateway configured in a User Agent's HTTP Proxy Policy. However, the XML Firewall service is not designed to use the CONNECT method for SSL tunneling.
per_question.reference: Yes, IBM DataPower Gateway Appliance does support the HTTP CONNECT method. Specifically, for SSL proxy requests, IBM DataPower Gateway Appliance supports the HTTP CONNECT method for SSL tunneling when using a Multi-Protocol Gateway and configuring in a User Agent's HTTP Proxy Policy.

per_question.user_input:
Datacap on Citrix  Hi All,

Can we operate Datacap thin clients on citrix?
retrieved_contexts: Some customers successfully operate Datacap thick clients in remote sites using Citrix or other remote access technology. IBM has not tested or sought certification with Citrix, and does not provide support for Citrix. If you deploy Datacap clients on Citrix and encounter issues, IBM may require you to reproduce the issues outside of Citrix as part of the investigation.

WAN LAN architecture deployment TECHNOTE (FAQ)

QUESTION
 What are the best practices for deploying Datacap servers and clients on a wide area network (WAN)? 

ANSWER
Remote users that access Datacap over a WAN can use Taskmaster Web-based "thin clients", or FastDoc Capture operating in offline mode. Datacap thick clients (DotScan, DotEdit) and utilities (NENU, Fingerprint Maintenance Tool) require LAN communication speeds and low latency for responsive performance.

1. On client system open a Command Prompt window 
 2. Type
    cd c:\Datacap\Taskmaster 
 3. Then type
    dcskey.exe -i 
 4. If you are still unable to connect then Remove the client system from the domain 
 5. On the client delete the local folder of the domain user 
 6. Reconnect the client to the domain 
 7. Log into client with domain user 
 8. Repeat steps 1 through 4 and connect with DotEdit and DotScan
. Prerequisites for IBM® Datacap Version 9.1.3 Interim Fix 002

If you’re publishing it as an application, advise users to do a File > Exit to cleanly close the ICA session and to avoid leaving ghost sessions on the XenApp server. You may also want to check with Citrix to see if they have suggestions on handling cases where ICA sessions are terminated but the processes are still running.
per_question.response: Yes, customers can successfully operate Datacap thick clients in remote sites using Citrix or other remote access technology. However, IBM has not tested or certified Datacap with Citrix and does not provide support for it.
per_question.reference: Based on the provided context, Datacap thin clients can be operated on Citrix. However, it is mentioned that IBM has not tested or sought certification with Citrix, and does not provide support for Citrix. If any issues are encountered while deploying Datacap clients on Citrix, IBM may require you to reproduce the issues outside of Citrix as part of the investigation.
